title: panic upon exiting from `podman unshare`
body:
### Issue Description

I ran podman unshare yesterday (did `podman pull`, `podman mount` etc). My ~/.local/share/containers is a symlink to an external drive, which I have unmounted before putting the laptop to sleep.

This morning I exited from `podman unshare`, while the external drive was not connected. Upon exiting, I got the following panic:

```
[root@kir-rhat check-payload]$ 
exit
panic: creating lock file directory: mkdir /home/kir/.local/share/containers: file exists

goroutine 1 [running]:
panic({0x55e3083edac0, 0xc000bf4020})
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman unshare
2. ???
3. Ctrl-D


### Describe the results you received

See above

### Describe the results you expected

Perhaps downgrade the panic to an error.

### podman info output

```yaml
[kir@kir-rhat check-payload]$ podman info
Error: opening database /home/kir/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db: open /home/kir/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db: no such file or directory
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Fedora 38
podman-4.5.1-1.fc38.x86_64

### Additional information

I'm on PTO so can't look into it this week.

comments.body:
Ok removing the underlying storage while podman is running is pretty bad and untested of course.
Should we handle it better? I think so but looking at your stack trace this seems difficult.

The panic comes out of the c/storage file locking code and the lock API has almost no error handling. `.Lock()` `.Unlock()` do not return errors so the only choice it has to ignore or panic for internal errors. If we have to add error handling for each lock that complicates the code quite a lot and even then it is not sure how to recover other returning the error. So in that case other than the bad ux a panic is likely not any worse.

And in general I would expect pretty much all code paths in podman to cause problems not just the locks here.
Agree this is not much we can do for this failure.
